heading: Information access by the Comptroller General of the United States
label: 3308 Information access by the Comptroller General of the United States
notes: References in TextThe National Security Act of 1947, referred to in subsec. (a)(3)(B), is , , which is classified principally to chapter 44 (§ 3001 et seq.) of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables.act July 26, 1947, ch. 34361 Stat. 495
CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 403–9 of this title
DefinitionFor definition of “intelligence community”, see , set out as a note under .section 2 of Pub. L. 111–259section 3003 of this title
amendments: References in TextThe National Security Act of 1947, referred to in subsec. (a)(3)(B), is , , which is classified principally to chapter 44 (§ 3001 et seq.) of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables.act July 26, 1947, ch. 34361 Stat. 495

children.heading: Requirement for directive
children.label: 1 Requirement for directive
children.text: The Director of National Intelligence, in consultation with the Comptroller General of the United States, shall issue a written directive governing the access of the Comptroller General to information in the possession of an element of the intelligence community.

children.heading: Amendment to directive
children.label: 2 Amendment to directive
children.text: The Director of National Intelligence, in consultation with the Comptroller General, may issue an amendment to the directive issued under paragraph (1) at any time the Director determines such an amendment is appropriate.

children.label: B
children.text: the National Security Act of 1947 ( et seq.).50 U.S.C. 401

children.heading: Requirement for confidentiality
children.label: 1 Requirement for confidentiality
children.text: The Comptroller General of the United States shall ensure that the level of confidentiality of information made available to the Comptroller General pursuant to the directive issued under subsection (a)(1) or an amendment to such directive issued under subsection (a)(2) is not less than the level of confidentiality of such information required of the head of the element of the intelligence community from which such information was obtained.

children.heading: Penalties for unauthorized disclosure
children.label: 2 Penalties for unauthorized disclosure
children.text: An officer or employee of the Government Accountability Office shall be subject to the same statutory penalties for unauthorized disclosure or use of such information as an officer or employee of the element of the intelligence community from which such information was obtained.

children.heading: Submission of directive
children.label: 1 Submission of directive
children.text: The directive issued under subsection (a)(1) shall be submitted to Congress by the Director of National Intelligence, together with any comments of the Comptroller General of the United States, no later than .May 1, 2011

children.heading: Submission of amendment
children.label: 2 Submission of amendment
children.text: Any amendment to such directive issued under subsection (a)(2) shall be submitted to Congress by the Director, together with any comments of the Comptroller General.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: d Effective date
children.text: The directive issued under subsection (a)(1) and any amendment to such directive issued under subsection (a)(2) shall take effect 60 days after the date such directive or amendment is submitted to Congress under subsection (c), unless the Director determines that for reasons of national security the directive or amendment should take effect sooner.
